name: Mom Stern's Noodle Kugel
recipeIngredient: 4 apples, peeled and sliced into small wedges
450 grams wide egg noodles (one box), cooked according to package directions
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the apples and slice them into small wedges.
2. Beat the eggs until foamy. Gradually add sugar while beating well.
3. Cook the egg noodles according to package instructions, then drain.
4. Melt the butter in a 9x13 inch metal baking pan (20x30 cm). Swirl it around to coat the pan, then pour into the egg mixture.
5. Add baking powder and vanilla extract to the egg mixture. Mix well, then slowly add drained noodles, apples, and raisins. Mix thoroughly until all noodles are coated and appear gooey.
6. Pour the egg/noodle mixture into the metal pan. Sprinkle cinnamon on top if desired. Bake at 220°C for about 45 minutes to an hour, or until the top is lightly browned and crispy. The kugel should feel springy when touched and not be wet.
7. Let cool, cut into squares, and serve.
Note: Muller's egg noodles work well for this recipe. This serves approximately 12 very hungry people (knoshers) or 16 regular portions. Leftovers can be wrapped in aluminum foil and stored in the freezer for up to two months. If it is too sweet, substitute with peaches or other fruit instead of raisins. Consuming more than 2 pieces per day may cause discomfort.
